sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing shortness of breath upon exertion, with a history of coughing up blood. He also reports intermittent episodes of high blood sugar, occasionally experiencing both high and low readings, and has been inconsistently taking his norvasc. He describes a past knee problem with no current swelling. He reports waking up with shortness of breath and feeling short of breath when he returns home.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Xygen saturation is 94% on clinic pulse oximetry. No jugular vein distention, no cervical lymphadenopathy, no carotid bruits. Lung exam reveals bilateral fine crackles and rhonchi. Heart exam reveals a two-plus systolic ejection murmur. Lower extremity exam reveals one plus nonpitting edema bilaterally. Hemoglobin A1c was 7.0% previously.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient is a 57-year-old male presenting with shortness of breath, cough with blood, and elevated blood sugar. He has a history of diabetes, hypertension, and asthma. A chest x-ray and blood work are indicated to evaluate for potential pneumonia or pulmonary embolism.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order a chest x-ray and blood work, including a CBC and CMP. Send the patient to the emergency department for a CT scan of the chest to evaluate for blood clots. Increase metformin to 750mg twice daily. Hold norvasc until blood pressure is better controlled. Schedule a follow-up appointment in three weeks to discuss diabetes management.
